title: PokéAPI Resources, URL API, Pagination Shapes, and Fair-Use Policy · Transfer Build
summary: An independent museum program receives a different audience, command script, unknown JSON payload, cache age, async schedule, accessibility need, Node platform, and injected failure and transfers pokéapi resources, url api, pagination shapes, and fair-use policy into a changed fixed-origin typed PokéAPI request policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of fixed-origin typed PokéAPI request policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Hold scheme and authority in trusted configuration and map commands only to enumerated PokéAPI resource paths.
Validate resource kind, numeric identifier or canonical constrained name, and preserve requested versus resolved identity.
Use URL and URLSearchParams for encoded path and limit or offset values while rejecting slash, control, oversized, and unexpected input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 2656 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit fixed-origin PokéAPI URLs, validated pagination shapes, and fair-use request budgets; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Hold scheme and authority in trusted configuration and map commands only to enumerated PokéAPI resource paths.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-ts-fair-use-contract-o4gman" that uses case 2485 to admit fixed-origin PokéAPI URLs, validated pagination shapes, and fair-use request budgets. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real signals, leave timers or Promise rejections, or claim native runtime, load, accessibility, packaging, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with TypeScript 7.0.2, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface where required, Node 24.18.0, original unknown fixtures, controlled fetch and stream adapters, emitted-runtime and subprocess tests, temporary files, package inspection, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: pokedex-ts-fair-use-contract-o4gman.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: pokedex-ts-fair-use-contract-o4gman(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_pokedex_ts_fair_use_contract_o4gman\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fixedOrigins !== 1)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?validatedInputs < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pageLimit < 1 \|\| pageLimit > 100)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?concurrentRequests < 1 \|\| concurrentRequests > 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Pokedex Ts Fair Use Contract to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Pokedex Ts Fair Use Contract.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using cache and requested plus specific case evidence.
